test_cases.description: Decision.as_lowercase() returns lowercase string representation for each variant
test_cases.input_description: Decision enum variants: Allow, Deny, Ask, Gate, Ensure

test_cases.description: default_gate_within() returns the expected default value
default_ensure_timeout() returns the expected default value
default_version() returns the expected default value
default_allow() returns Decision.Allow
from_config() compiles valid regex patterns successfully
test_cases.input_description: PolicyConfig with 2 rules: valid regex patterns '^file_.*' and '.*\.sh$'
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns CompiledPolicy with 2 compiled rules in original order

test_cases.description: from_config() silently filters out rules with invalid regex patterns
test_cases.input_description: PolicyConfig with 3 rules: one valid, one with invalid regex '(unclosed', one valid
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns CompiledPolicy with 2 valid rules only, preserving order

test_cases.description: from_config() handles PolicyConfig with empty rules list
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns CompiledPolicy with empty rules, default_action preserved

test_cases.description: evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates contains() function
evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates param_eq() function
evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates param_gt() function
evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates param_lt() function
evaluate_condition() handles 'true' literal
evaluate_condition() handles 'false' literal
evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates not() function
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Ok(true) since 'safe' is not in tool_name

test_cases.description: evaluate_condition() correctly evaluates or() function
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Ok(true) since first operand matches

test_cases.description: evaluate_condition() returns Err for unknown condition function
evaluate_condition() returns Err for invalid regex in matches()
evaluate_condition() returns Err for non-numeric threshold in param_gt()
evaluate() returns decision from first matching rule
test_cases.input_description: Policy with 2 rules matching same tool, first denies, second allows

test_cases.description: evaluate() returns default_action when no rules match
test_cases.input_description: Policy with rules that don't match the tool call

test_cases.description: evaluate() sets matched_locked=true for locked rules
evaluate() resolves Gate action to Allow when vault has recent action

mock_dependencies.vault: Mock vault with has_recent_allowed_action('plan', 50) returning true

test_cases.input_description: Gate rule requiring prior 'plan' action, vault has it

test_cases.description: evaluate() resolves Gate action to Deny when vault is None (fail-closed)
evaluate() returns Ensure decision with ensure_config populated
evaluate() always populates evaluation_time_us
evaluate() does not match rule when condition evaluation returns Err
test_cases.input_description: Rule with invalid condition, followed by matching rule
test_cases.expected_output_description: Falls through to second rule, returns Allow

test_cases.description: resolve_gate() returns true when vault has matching recent action

mock_dependencies.vault: Mock vault with has_recent_allowed_action('approve', 20) returning true

test_cases.input_description: GateConfig requiring 'approve' within 20, vault has it

test_cases.description: resolve_gate() returns false when vault doesn't have matching action

mock_dependencies.vault: Mock vault with has_recent_allowed_action('approve', 20) returning false

test_cases.input_description: GateConfig requiring 'approve', vault doesn't have it

test_cases.description: resolve_gate() returns false when vault is None (fail-closed)
load_policy() successfully loads and compiles policy from valid YAML file
test_cases.setup_description: Create temporary YAML file with valid policy
test_cases.input_description: Path to valid policy YAML file
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns CompiledPolicy with rules from file

test_cases.description: load_policy() returns default_policy() when file doesn't exist
load_policy() returns default_policy() when YAML is malformed
test_cases.setup_description: Create temporary file with malformed YAML
test_cases.input_description: Path to file with invalid YAML

test_cases.description: load_policy_config() successfully loads PolicyConfig from valid YAML
test_cases.setup_description: Create temporary YAML file with valid policy structure
test_cases.input_description: Path to valid policy YAML file

test_cases.description: load_policy_config() returns Err when file doesn't exist
load_policy_config() returns Err when YAML is malformed
test_cases.setup_description: Create temporary file with malformed YAML (unclosed brackets, etc.)
test_cases.input_description: Path to file with invalid YAML syntax

test_cases.description: validate_policy() returns empty Vec for valid PolicyConfig
test_cases.input_description: PolicyConfig with valid regex, known conditions, proper Gate/Ensure configs

test_cases.description: validate_policy() returns error for invalid regex pattern
test_cases.input_description: PolicyConfig with rule containing invalid regex '(unclosed'
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Vec with regex error message
assertions: any('regex' in err.lower() for err in result)

test_cases.description: validate_policy() returns error for unknown condition function
test_cases.input_description: PolicyConfig with rule using unknown condition 'bad_fn()'
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Vec with unknown condition error
assertions: any('condition' in err.lower() or 'unknown' in err.lower() for err in result)

test_cases.description: validate_policy() returns error when Gate action missing gate config
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Vec with missing gate config error
assertions: any('gate' in err.lower() for err in result)

test_cases.description: validate_policy() returns error when Ensure action missing ensure config
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Vec with missing ensure config error
assertions: any('ensure' in err.lower() for err in result)

test_cases.description: validate_ensure_check_name() accepts safe script names
validate_ensure_check_name() rejects empty name
validate_ensure_check_name() rejects name with path separator
validate_ensure_check_name() rejects name with '..'
validate_ensure_check_name() rejects name with null byte
validate_ensure_check_name() rejects name with control characters
test_cases.input_description: Name containing control character (< 0x20, except tab)

test_cases.description: resolve_ensure_script_path() returns canonicalized path for valid script
test_cases.input_description: Valid check name 'test_check.sh' in existing .signet/checks/ directory
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns Ok(PathBuf) with canonical path in .signet/checks/

test_cases.description: resolve_ensure_script_path() returns Err for invalid check name
resolve_ensure_script_path() returns Err when script doesn't exist
test_cases.input_description: Valid check name but file doesn't exist
assertions: 'resolve' in result.unwrap_err().lower() or 'not found' in result.unwrap_err().lower()

test_cases.description: resolve_ensure_script_path() returns Err when canonical path escapes checks directory
test_cases.setup_description: Create symlink pointing outside checks directory
test_cases.input_description: Symlink or other mechanism causing path to escape .signet/checks/

test_cases.description: self_protection_rules() returns exactly 7 rules (github_identity_guard moved to default_policy as unlocked)
self_protection_rules() returns all rules with locked=true
self_protection_rules() has protect_checks_dir as first rule
self_protection_rules() covers all required protection areas
test_cases.expected_output_description: Rules protect: checks dir, signet dir, binary, hook config, symlink, process, preflight, github
assertions: any('checks' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('signet_dir' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('binary' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('hook' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('symlink' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('process' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('preflight' in rule.name for rule in result)
any('github' in rule.name or 'identity' in rule.name for rule in result)

test_cases.description: default_policy() has self_protection_rules at the beginning
test_cases.expected_output_description: First 7 rules are self-protection rules with locked=true

test_cases.description: default_policy() has default_action = Allow
default_policy() includes standard safety rules beyond self-protection
test_cases.expected_output_description: Policy includes rules for plan-before-code, rm protection, force-push, etc.

test_cases.description: split_at_top_level() splits at separator when depth is 0
split_at_top_level() ignores separator inside parentheses
test_cases.input_description: String 'or(a, b), c' with separator ', ' (should split after closing paren)

test_cases.description: split_at_top_level() returns None when separator not found at depth 0
test_cases.input_description: String 'or(a, b)' with separator ', ' (only inside parens)

test_cases.description: split_at_top_level() returns None for empty separator
strip_fn() extracts arguments from function call
strip_fn() returns None when function name doesn't match
strip_fn() returns None when parentheses are missing or unmatched
extract_quoted() extracts content from double-quoted string
extract_quoted() extracts content from single-quoted string
extract_quoted() returns None for unquoted string
extract_quoted() returns None for mismatched quotes
param_str() extracts string parameter value
param_str() returns empty string when field not found
param_str() returns empty string when field is not a string
param_f64() extracts numeric parameter value
param_f64() parses numeric string
param_f64() returns 0.0 when field not found
param_f64() returns 0.0 when value cannot be parsed
